For other uses, see [Commune (disambiguation)](/wiki/Commune_(disambiguation) "Commune (disambiguation)").

A **commune** ([Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language"): *comuna*, IPA: [[koˈmuna]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Spanish "Help:IPA/Spanish")) is the smallest administrative subdivision in [Chile](/wiki/Chile "Chile"). It may contain [cities](/wiki/City "City"), [towns](/wiki/Town "Town"), [villages](/wiki/Village "Village"), [hamlets](/wiki/Hamlet_(place) "Hamlet (place)") as well as [rural](/wiki/Rural "Rural") areas. In highly populated areas, such as [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago,_Chile "Santiago, Chile"), [Valparaíso](/wiki/Valpara%C3%ADso "Valparaíso") and [Concepción](/wiki/Concepci%C3%B3n,_Chile "Concepción, Chile"), a conurbation may be broken into several communes. In sparsely populated areas, conversely, a commune may cover a substantial rural area together with several settled areas which could range from hamlets to towns or cities.

The term "commune" is ambiguous in English, but the word is commonly used in translation for "comuna", although with some controversy among translators. A comuna is similar to a "county" in Anglo-American usage and practice, and may be more universally understood as a "municipality".

Each commune or municipality is governed by a [directly elected](/wiki/Direct_election "Direct election") body known as a municipal council (*concejo municipal*) consisting of a mayor (*alcalde*) and a group of councillors (*concejales*), for a period of four years. The communal civil service administration is known as the [municipality](/wiki/Municipality "Municipality") (*[municipalidad](/wiki/Municipalidad "Municipalidad")*) and is headquartered at the mayor's office (*alcaldía*). According to Chilean law, a single municipality may administer one or more communes, though currently, the only such case is the municipality of [Cabo de Hornos](/wiki/Cabo_de_Hornos,_Chile "Cabo de Hornos, Chile"), which administers the communes of [Antártica](/wiki/Ant%C3%A1rtica_(commune) "Antártica (commune)") and [Cabo de Hornos](/wiki/Cabo_de_Hornos,_Chile "Cabo de Hornos, Chile").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Chile's 346 communes are grouped into 56 [provinces](/wiki/Provinces_of_Chile "Provinces of Chile") (*provincia*, pl. *provincias*), which are themselves grouped into 16 [regions](/wiki/Regions_of_Chile "Regions of Chile") (*región*, pl. *regiones*).
